behavior_summary: U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch runs a short 'hatching' sequence for the object the process is attached to: it freezes the player avatar and temporarily pins the camera, invokes HYDROS-specific routines on the object, emits a bark line, optionally starts a LightningBolt effect, spawns a temporary Item instance at the object's location and animates it through a few frames, destroys the temporary item, applies a randomized hit/impulse to create motion, waits for short timers between steps, and finally unfreezes the avatar.
The sequence uses multiple suspend/wait points (spawned helper processes) to pace animations/effects, and randomization (FREE::randomInRange, urandom) to vary hit magnitude/direction.
A conditional branch triggers an external LightningBolt process and related SFX when a small flag/condition is set; otherwise the routine continues with the item spawn/animation path.
notable_control_flow: Starts by calling setAvatarInStasis(0x01) to freeze the avatar and spawns FREE::cameraFollowAvatar to focus the camera on the U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch location.
Calls two object-specific routines (HYDROS::func0DDB then HYDROS::func12B0) with suspend points between them to drive the U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch animation/state changes.
Conditionally spawns process LightningBolt and plays a SFX branch when a local/flag condition equals 0x01.
Creates an Item (Item::create), places it in the world (Item::pop), then loops incrementing local3 from 0 while <= 4 calling Item::setFrame and waiting (via FREE::func1C19) to animate frames.

After frame animation the temporary Item is destroyed (Item::destroy) and Item::receiveHit is called with randomized parameters to impart motion; additional waits occur before cleanup and setAvatarInStasis(0x00) to unfreeze.
open_questions: Which exact in-game flag or state controls the LightningBolt branch (metadata shows a condition but not the flag name) — this is inferred but not explicitly named.
Precise effects and meanings of HYDROS::func0DDB and HYDROS::func12B0 are unknown; they likely perform object-specific animation/state updates.
Numeric meanings of several magic constants (sound ids, small process ids, argument values) are inferred from context but not confirmed.
The exact role of the temporary Item instance (local2) — visual U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch, spawned creature, or debris — is inferred from usage but not explicitly stated.

main_function.name: U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch
main_function.purpose: Perform the hatching visual/effect sequence for the HYDROS object this process runs on: freeze avatar/camera, run object-specific animations, optionally spawn lightning, create and animate a temporary Item for visuals, apply randomized physics to it, then cleanup and unfreeze the avatar.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the 'U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch' process runs on the item/object (process type 0x0210) — typically when the object reaches its U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch-triggered lifecycle state.
Implicit: triggered by the engine when this object's U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch process is started (no explicit menu or player input).

function_map.name: U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch
function_map.role: Controls the U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch sequence for the HYDROS object; coordinates avatar stasis, camera, object-specific routines, temporary item animation, effects, and cleanup.

function_map.name: setAvatarInStasis
function_map.role: Engine call to freeze/unfreeze the player avatar; used at sequence start/end.

function_map.name: FREE::cameraFollowAvatar
function_map.role: Adjust camera to focus on the avatar/object during the sequence.

function_map.name: HYDROS::func0DDB
function_map.role: HYDROS-specific routine invoked early in the U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch; likely updates object animation/state.

function_map.name: HYDROS::func12B0
function_map.role: HYDROS-specific routine invoked later in the U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch; likely continues or finalizes animation/state change.

function_map.name: process LightningBolt
function_map.role: Optional spawned effect/process (lightning) triggered by a conditional branch during U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch.

function_map.name: Item::create / Item::pop / Item::setFrame / Item::destroy / Item::receiveHit
function_map.role: Item lifecycle and visual handling for the temporary spawn used to display the U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch animation and receive physics impulses.

function_map.name: FREE::func1C19 / FREE::func1C8B / FREE::randomInRange / urandom
function_map.role: Engine helper routines for short waits/timers and randomization used throughout the timed animation and impulse calculations.

function_map.name: playSFX
function_map.role: Plays sound effects at key moments during the U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch sequence.

external_calls.purpose: Freeze (0x01) at start and unfreeze (0x00) at end so the player cannot move during the U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch animation.
Spawn a camera-follow process focused at the U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch coordinates to frame the visual sequence.
Invoke object-specific U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch behavior/animation early in the sequence.
Continue/finish object-specific U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch behavior/animation later in the sequence.
Spawn a small helper process (likely a short wait/effect) used between animation steps.
Conditionally start a lightning/effect process when a branch condition equals 0x01.
Play SFX at key moments (e.g., during the lightning branch and impact), enhancing the U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch visuals.
Create a temporary item instance used to visualize the U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch/spawned object.
Place the created item into the world at coordinates derived from the HYDROS object's position (z + offset, y, x).
Step the item's animation frame in a short loop (local3 = 0..4) to animate the U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch.
Spawn short waits/timers between frame updates and after the final hit to pace the sequence.
Destroy the temporary item once its visual role is complete.
Apply a randomized hit/impulse to the spawned item to produce motion/scatter as part of the U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch effect.
Compute a random magnitude/value used as part of the receiveHit parameters.
Generate random direction/seed values for timing and hit direction.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_ENDHYDRO::hatch runs a short 'hatching' sequence for the object the process is attached to: it freezes the player avatar and temporarily pins the camera, invokes HYDROS-specific routines on the object, emits a bark line, optionally starts a LightningBolt effect, spawns a temporary Item instance at the object's location and animates it through a few frames, destroys the temporary item, applies a randomized hit/impulse to create motion, waits for short timers between steps, and finally unfreezes the avatar.
